sources.notes: Minimize popcount-related carry behavior after adding powers of two a limited number of times.
Writeup observes carry count as popcount decreases; for large k popcount can be reduced to 1, otherwise solve k <= 30 with digit DP.

annotation.summary: After at most k additions of powers of two to n, minimize the resulting popcount/carry-chain objective.
annotation.constraints: Large k has a direct lowbit-greedy outcome; the remaining hard case has k <= 30 and can be handled by bit DP.
annotation.core_idea: A carry operation corresponds to decreasing popcount. Repeatedly adding the current lowbit can merge set bits, and when k is large enough the number can be made a power of two. For small k, process bits with a digit DP that tracks how many additions have been used and the carry/popcount effect.
annotation.complexity: O(k * log n * small_state) for k <= 30; O(log n) for the large-k shortcut.
tricks: large-k shortcut to power of two

solution_variants.name: carry-digit-dp
solution_variants.summary: Use the lowbit shortcut for large k and digit DP over binary bits for the bounded-k case.

tags.evidence: The bounded case is solved by DP over binary digits and carry/addition usage.
The key invariant is binary popcount and carry behavior under adding powers of two.
Carries are interpreted as moments when popcount decreases.
tags.description: Use popcount changes or parity of set bits as the main invariant.
tags.created_reason: Popcount-based invariants are common in bitwise DP and games.
